agent2_output.title: Wi-Fi 7 Technical Analysis: Advancements Over Wi-Fi 6E
agent2_output.body: Wi-Fi 7 represents a substantial technological advancement over its predecessor, Wi-Fi 6E, introducing key features that enhance wireless connectivity and performance. [1] A primary distinction lies in multi-link operation, a capability allowing devices to concurrently connect to multiple access points or frequency bands. Wi-Fi 7 has a maximum speed of up to 46 Gbps, while Wi-Fi 6E has a maximum speed of up to 9.6 Gbps. [3] Still exhibits limitations in managing connections across multiple bands and its maximum data rates. Wi-Fi 7 addresses these deficiencies while maintaining compatibility with the 6 GHz spectrum, effectively making it a more powerful iteration of Wi-Fi 6E.
